reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes the informal claim. The universal "any two" is captured by the universally-quantified Types G and H. "Nonabelian" is correctly expressed as ∃ x y, x*y ≠ y*x (the logical negation of the commutativity axiom ∀ x y, x*y = y*x). The hypotheses p > q, Nat.Prime p, Nat.Prime q, q ∣ p - 1, and card G = card H = p*q all match the informal premises literally. The conclusion Nonempty (G ≃* H) is the standard Mathlib formulation of "G and H are isomorphic as groups". The Fintype instances are not extra narrowing hypotheses but are natural prerequisites for asserting finite cardinality p*q.
